name: Pepper-And-Tomato Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Olive or sunflower seed oil
4 Garlic cloves, peeled and coarsely chopped
1 lb Red peppers or pimientos, thinly sliced
1 lb Very ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped (juice reserved)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove stems, seeds, and veins from ancho chiles if using. Tear the flesh into a few large pieces and cover with 1 cup water in a small saucepan. Bring to boil, then simmer for 20 minutes. Puree in a blender.
2. Heat oil in a pan and add onions, bay leaves, cloves, coriander, and parsley. Cook over medium heat until the onions have begun to soften, about several minutes.
3. Add salt, peppers, and cabbage. Stir to combine and coat vegetables with oil.
4. Add 1/2 cup water or stock, cover the pan, and cook over low heat for 10 minutes until the vegetables have wilted.
5. Stir in 1/4 cup pureed chiles or 1 to 2 teaspoons chile powder.
6. Add tomatoes, their juice, and remaining water or stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer very slowly for 30 minutes.
7. When finished cooking, remove bay leaves. Puree the soup until completely blended.
8. Return it to the pan and season with salt, if needed. Add more chile if desired.
9. Serve soup with a swirl of creme fraiche and sprinkling of chopped cilantro.
